title: Caleana parvula
taxonomy: Caleana parvula is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Caleana, which is a group of terrestrial orchids found in Australia. The specific classification of Caleana parvula is Caleana parvula, a species within the orchid family. Caleana parvula is further classified as a member of the tribe Diurideae, which is a subfamily of orchids within the family Orchidaceae. This classification reflects Caleana parvula's unique characteristics and its relationship to other orchid species.

intro: Caleana parvula, commonly known as the fairy orchid, is a terrestrial orchid native to Australia, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and unique appearance. The fairy orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
